personSummary.value: Former carpenter who tends to wake early and looks for purposeful workshop-like tasks. Safe polishing, sorting screws and washers, and simple step-by-step prompts help him feel respected. He becomes distracted or upset when multiple people speak at once. Framing morning medication as the first step before a familiar handrail polishing routine worked well.

value.description: Early waking with workshop questions has been calmed by safe polishing tasks.

citations.quote: small cloth to polish the wooden handrail

value.description: Conflicting simultaneous instructions led to raised voice and chair movement.

citations.quote: Two staff members gave different morning instructions
one staff member repeated a single task

value: Offer a safe object to inspect or polish during early morning restlessness.
Give one step at a time, with only one staff member speaking.
Use sorting tasks with the activity box when he needs focused engagement.

citations.quote: safe object to inspect or polish

value: Two staff members speaking or giving different instructions at once.
Early morning uncertainty about whether his work has started.

citations.quote: gave different morning instructions at the same time
asked whether the workshop had opened

value.situation: Early morning waking before routine begins
value.approach: Give him a cloth and a safe handrail-polishing task before moving into care.

citations.quote: became calmer after staff gave him a small cloth

value.situation: Multiple staff are present during care
value.approach: Designate one speaker and repeat a single task before touching the chair or table.

citations.quote: one staff member repeated a single task
first step before his handrail polishing routine

handoffBrief.value: Shigeru Kobayashi does best with purposeful carpenter-style tasks, one speaker, and step-by-step prompts; avoid overlapping instructions, especially in the early morning.

value.claim: Watch early-morning agitation when more than one staff member gives instructions.
